abstract: Extracting structured knowledge from scientific literature remains a significant hurdle for machine learning. In this work, we introduce a straightforward approach for joint named entity recognition and relation extraction by fine-tuning pretrained large language models (LLMs), specifically GPT-3 and Llama-2. We demonstrate that these models can be trained to transform unstructured text into complex, high-fidelity records of scientific knowledge across three distinct materials chemistry tasks: identifying dopant-host relationships, cataloging metal-organic frameworks, and extracting general information regarding composition, phase, and application. Our method processes information at both the sentence and paragraph levels, offering the flexibility to output data as natural English sentences or structured formats like JSON objects. This framework provides an accessible and scalable pathway for researchers to compile large, specialized databases from the vast body of published research papers.
